params.name: Theory of Population
params.description:
On Nov 8, 1789 AD, Malthus released his revolutionary population theory.
Malthus, a famous demographer, and economist in Britain published a theory on population growth. It made two assumptions that "humans must rely on food" and "passion between men and women will be constant". He proposed that the population will grow exponentially, but food production can only increase in an arithmetic manner. When population growth exceeds the food supply, it will result in increased poverty. This theory …
